name: Ground Segment Support System Exposure + Credential Abuse
description: Simulates adversary targeting of a ground segment support system: initial reconnaissance of mission config artifacts, credential harvesting from exposed config files, auth abuse against privileged ops accounts, lateral staging of telemetry/command data, and anti-forensics pressure on ground system logs. Maps directly to SDL SDA Mission Engineering threat model — specifically the attack surface of ground-to-space link control infrastructure.

attck_mapping.T1078: Valid Accounts — credential abuse against ground segment ops user
attck_mapping.T1552.001: Credentials in Files — harvest from mission config artifacts
attck_mapping.T1110.001: Brute Force: Password Guessing — repeated auth failures before success
attck_mapping.T1005: Data from Local System — staging of telemetry/command data files
attck_mapping.T1070.006: Indicator Removal: Timestomp — anti-forensics on ground system logs
attck_mapping.T1059.004: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell — remote exec artifacts
attck_mapping.T1547.013: Boot or Logon Autostart: XDG Autostart Entries — persistence path mutation

sdl_role_alignment.requirement: Cyber experience with Space Systems and understanding of Threats and Cyber Defense
sdl_role_alignment.maps_to: Demonstrates detection coverage for ground segment credential compromise and mission data staging — the exact class of incident SDL/SDA cyber mission engineers are expected to investigate and prevent
forensic_artifacts: Auth burst against svc-gssc-ops account (Ground Segment Support Center)
Mission config file modification (sensitive.conf → mission_config.conf analogue)
Persistence stub mimicking ground support daemon entry
Log truncation on auth.log to impede incident reconstruction

preconditions: monitored directories seeded with mission-analogue config files
success_criteria: auth burst against ops account surfaces as single correlated incident
mission config tamper chain reconstructable from file events
telemetry staging archive detected before cleanup
persistence stub detected in path-mutation telemetry
anti-forensics truncation differentiated from normal log rotation
full campaign maps to continuous MITRE ATT&CK kill chain
coverage gaps listed in final report for SDL T&E review
sdl_reporting_notes: This scenario is designed to produce the exact telemetry signature an SDL Cyber Mission Engineer would need to investigate following a ground segment compromise. The resulting execution report serves as a live SCTM validation artifact — expected detections vs. actual detections = measurable coverage gap score. Attach output to program cyber risk register as evidence of T&E.
